h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: Wait, if the words are identical, how much can I change the caption without making it look like I am inventing meaning?
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, I get it: the verb is the shelf, but I may be trying to make "no longer functioned" hold up a whole wall.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was on a trajectory of refining caption wording incrementally, testing which verbs the archive could license, expecting to arrive at a permitted phrase.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Put the official line in two mouths. Before donors, it works as policy defence. After the cuts are named, in a workers' meeting, it may work as warning, but only if the source note gives the hinges: meeting, pressure, audience, sequence.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In response to L2's slippage on 'job' and the risk of guessing intent, T2 abandons abstract principle and introduces a concrete dual-mouth demonstration plus a verb-testing protocol, shifting the representation from conceptual analogy to operational procedure.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: That is the new centre: not how far the caption may move the quote, but what change of public position the source note can prove. So change the task before print. Put the source note beside four verbs: "repeated" "placed before" "shifted" "no longer functioned"
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T3 directly adopts L3's reframed centre — from caption-movement anxiety to provable change of public position — and converts it into a new ranked-verb task that the learner did not have before the pivot.
